description: census designated place in Box Elder County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Thatcher is a census-designated place in Box Elder County, Utah, United States.

description: county in northwestern Utah, United States
description_wiki: Box Elder County is a county at the northwestern corner of Utah, United States.

surface_forms: Air Corps of the United States
description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

surface_forms: the four that were killed out of the five brothers
description: American military siblings
description_wiki: The Borgstrom brothers were four American siblings, including twin brothers, killed over a six-month period during World War II.

description: 1998 film directed by Steven Spielberg
description_wiki: Saving Private Ryan is a 1998 American epic war film directed by Steven Spielberg and written by Robert Rodat.

description: city in Box Elder County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Tremonton is a city in Box Elder County, Utah, United States.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: city in and county seat of Wichita County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Wichita Falls ( WITCH-ih-tah) is a city in and the county seat of Wichita County, Texas, United States.

description: city in Bexar, Comal, and Medina counties in Texas, United States, that is the seat of Bexar County
description_wiki: San Antonio ( SAN an-TOH-nee-oh; Spanish for "Saint Anthony") is a city in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: 1917-2011 United States military air base in San Antonio, Texas, USA
description_wiki: Brooks Air Force Base was a United States Air Force facility located in San Antonio, Texas, 7 miles (11 km) southeast of Downtown San Antonio.

description: military facility in San Antonio, Texas
description_wiki: Kelly Field (formerly Kelly Air Force Base) (IATA: SKF, ICAO: KSKF, FAA LID: SKF) is a Joint-Use facility located in San Antonio, Texas.

description: historic place in Bexar County, Texas
description_wiki: Randolph Air Force Base (IATA: RND, ICAO: KRND, FAA LID: RND) is a United States Air Force base located in Bexar County, Texas, (14.8 miles (23.8 km) east-northeast of Downtown San Antonio).

description: American general officer holding the ranks of General of the Army and General of the Air Force (1886-1950)
description_wiki: Henry Harley "Hap" Arnold (25 June 1886 – 15 January 1950) was an American general officer holding the ranks of General of the Army and later, General of the Air Force.

description: 1942-1945 United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 8th Armored Division was an armored division of the United States Army that served in the European Theater of World War II.

description: United States Army installation in Vernon Parish, Louisiana, United States, formerly named Fort Johnson
description_wiki: Fort Polk, formerly Fort Johnson, is a United States Army installation located in Vernon Parish, Louisiana, about 10 miles (15 km) east of Leesville and 30 miles (50 km) north of DeRidder in Beauregard Parish.

description: Semi-automatic rifle
description_wiki: The M1 Garand or M1 rifle is a semi-automatic rifle that was the service rifle of the U.S. Army during World War II and the Korean War.

description: medium machine gun
heavy machine gun
heavy machine gun
description_wiki: The M2 machine gun or Browning .50-caliber machine gun (informally, "Ma Deuce") is a heavy machine gun that was designed near the end of World War I by John Browning.

description_wiki: Mortar may refer to:

description: United States Army general (1885–1945)
description_wiki: George Smith Patton Jr.

description: 1942 medium tank family
description_wiki: The M4 Sherman, officially Medium Tank, M4, was the medium tank most widely used by the United States and Western Allies in World War II. The M4 Sherman was reliable and well adapted to mass production.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: city and major port in Hampshire, South-East England, United Kingdom
description_wiki: Southampton is a port city and unitary authority in Hampshire, England.

description: low mountain range in Belgium, France, Luxembourg and Germany
description_wiki: The Ardennes, also known as the Ardennes Forest or Forest of Ardennes, is a region of extensive forests, rough terrain, rolling hills and ridges primarily in Belgium and Luxembourg, extending into Germany and France.

description: German Field Marshal during World War II (1875-1953)
description_wiki: Karl Rudolf Gerd von Rundstedt (12 December 1875 – 24 February 1953) was a German Generalfeldmarschall (Field Marshal) in the Heer (Army) of Nazi Germany and Oberbefehlshaber West (Commander-in-Chief in the West) during World War II. At the end of the war, aged 69, with over 52 years of service, he was the Army's most senior officer.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.

description: region and former province in the western Netherlands
description_wiki: Holland is a geographical region and former province on the western coast of the Netherlands.

description: city, municipality and capital of province Limburg, the Netherlands
description_wiki: Maastricht ( MAH-strikht, US also  mah-STRIKHT, Dutch: [maːˈstrɪxt] ; Limburgish: Mestreech [məˈstʀeːx]) is a city and a municipality in the southeastern Netherlands.

description: city in the municipality with the same name, Netherlands
description_wiki: Roermond (Dutch pronunciation: [ruːrˈmɔnt] ; Limburgish: Remunj [ʀəˈmʏɲ] or Remuunj [ʀəˈmyːɲ]) is a city, municipality, and diocese in the Limburg province of the Netherlands.

description: river in Germany and the Netherlands
description_wiki: The Roer (Dutch: [ruːr] , Limburgish: [ʀuːʀ˦]) or Rur (German: [ʁuːɐ̯] ; French: Rour [ʁuʁ]) is a major river that flows through portions of Belgium, Germany and the Netherlands.
